item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I realized a while after publishing that it might give the wrong impression, so I completely understand your viewpoint. Thank you for the feedback, though!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That would explain my confused reaction when I read this piece. The initial impression that I came to is "there's this village of Jews in Latvia, and it appears that a disproportionate number of these villagers are perpetrators of really heinous crimes."

Since your motivation was to write a piece that implicitly condemned anti-Semitism, it's unfortunate that this piece gave me the impression that you were headed in the opposite direction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: thank you

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: good

item.title: Re: SCP-4531 — Silence encourages the tormentor, never the tormented.
item.content:encoded: Thank you! I'd also recommend SCP-5613 for other Jewish works, even though it's not my article.

item.title: Re: SCP-4531 — Silence encourages the tormentor, never the tormented.
item.content:encoded: Dude this was great. I'm also Jewish and I'm actually studying Judaic Studies for my major and you hit everything spot on. I really hope to see more Jewish inspired SCPs like this one. I'm sure you know about Kabbalah and the impact it can have on this site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 haunting

► spoilers ◄
►close◄

I have to say that I think the foundation is finding Jews that have done terrible things and introducing them to the anomaly so they have more -2 to collect.

I also assume the Mayor’s crime is redacted and causing such a large manifestation of -1 because they’re complicit in the Foundation utilizing this ritual for its own means, therefore the mayor is at least indirectly responsible for all the deaths being laid at the feet of those others who took part in the above logs… just my thoughts

►close◄

Anyway, amazing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You hit it right on the money! I appreciate how much you looked into this skip, and I'm glad that the symbolism didn't go over everyone's head

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ey I like this. This is a skip that the more you look into it, the more you enjoy the small details put into it. For such a small skip, its strangely compact with information.

+ show block
– hide block

I noticed that the number of pair of hands = number of deaths that person caused. Maybe the animal that the -2 instances take represents the crime as well? Fox for the sly, wolf for the brutal, mallards are notoriously rapey animals etc

The guy that killed the Foundation researchers had a body count of 31. 64 Hands appeared. One of them held a scale, I'm not quite sure what the appearance of those last two hands inquires, other than the fact this person will be judged for their actions.

The Mayor herself has spilled a lot of blood, she may have not specifically killed anyone, but her policies probably have.

And then theres the mysterious Project Tochacha and the Thaumiel status which puzzled me at first because why would the Foundation need to classify as Thaumiel status a small community that makes anomalies appear? But then I realized that the liquid itself is a thing that the Foundation would really appreciate to collect more of to interact with dangerous cognitohazardous anomalies. It doesn't know how to fabricate it, but it does know how to extract it.
Project Tochacha is the procedure in which the Foundation has figured out how to weaponize a tradition meant to expose the sins of the inhabitants to themselves and to make themselves repentant, and the Foundation found a way to maximize the creation process of this liquid by instigating and promoting a perverse mindset in the community, akin to the serpent telling Eve to eat the forbidden fruit.

The last line can be interpreted in a number of different ways. Either the Project found out an even better way of harvest, which is to introduce a person who has indirectly caused a lot of blood on their hands to the anomaly for gallons upon gallons of juice.
Either that or the Project received absolutely 0 liquid from this instance, which made them panic. Maybe the mayor is part of the Project and is the one explicitly perverting her community for the Foundation, which made the anomaly decide not to produce anything to attempt to disrupt the Mayor's mindset of harvest?

Either way, I enjoyed this one. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is about what I figured. It was subtle enough that I thought I missed a link to another document or something.

Spoilers
– hide block

Then the realization hit me after I looked back to the other references to project tochacha

item.title: Re: -1
item.content:encoded:
I don't typically break format unless I'm trying to convey something (4213 has no cusotm formatting, 4792 uses the threat level to subvert the reader shortly into the SCP), and that's no different here.

The intention of the large header, when I wrote this, was not to tell the reader "prepare for an epic quest unlike any other!" but rather to inform the reader (alongside the warning box, heavy redactions, and other minor details) that this anomaly is of great importance to the Foundation. Once the reader has this in mind, I envisioned it would help them deduce what exactly is behind the redactions, and why the Foundation is limiting information on a "need-to-know basis."

I completely understand why you'd respond the way you would to seeing that large introduction though, and I'm sorry that the intro ruined the entire piece for you. I'll take this feedback into consideration when designing SCPs in the future. Thank you for clarifying your downvote!

EDIT: Minor typo I noticed, like, months later lol

The article is fine. It's good.

The enormous warning header (which was not followed up on to a sufficient extent to justify it's biglyness), new classification system (which probably needs callbacks in this first week of it's existence so people get that it's a Thing), and full-width image, when put together, means I can't even see the meat of the article without scrolling on a full HD display, and that is too much mustard on the hot dog. These big elements are framing devices for the story, they set expectations and it's an additive thing, each thing you introduce, omit or alter necessitates a proportionally larger payoff at the end. Add in other more subtle alterations of the format (Addendum One, spelled out as such, centered) and the payoff is simply not there for me, sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1, I really liked this!

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded:
Copy-pasted from my answer to a similar question that Lucio asked above:

I wanted to do heads at first, but it would be kinda weird to envision these creatures walking around with heads on them. I went with hands because it represented the victims, sort of like a physical version of the phrase "you have blood on your hands." It also felt creepier.

Thank you! I'm glad you enjoyed the skip! ^-^
Was their an in or out of universe reason for all the symbolism involving hands (with the ultimate monster being all hands) or is it just there for creepy imagery?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Err, not exactly. I left a spoiler'd comment above with my intentions while writing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Feels to me like an SCP analogue to that "We Become What We Behold" computer….well it's not exactly a game per se.

A study of self-amplifying vicious circles of behavior. That's what I perceive here.

item.title: Re: SCP-4531 — Silence encourages the tormentor, never the tormented.
item.content:encoded:
I think you just summarized world history, dude.

Haha, unfortunately.

Also, is there any reason for the constant hand imagery, or is it just to make the SCP creepier?

I wanted to do heads at first, but it would be kinda weird to envision these creatures walking around with heads on them. I went with hands because it represented the victims, sort of like a physical version of the phrase "you have blood on your hands." It also felt creepier.

item.title: Re: SCP-4531 — Silence encourages the tormentor, never the tormented.
item.content:encoded:
[…] the fact that it even happened is a major telling sign at the way Jews are seen in the modern day, not including many, many other things.

I think you just summarized world history, dude.

I really liked the SCP, even if I don't fully understand it. Also, is there any reason for the constant hand imagery, or is it just to make the SCP creepier?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oh jeez that's more horrifying than what i thought and i still really dig how well you've done this

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Spoilers
Spoiled

Of course, I left the reader to piece together their own story, so any interpretation goes.

My interpretation while writing this piece was that the Foundation was specifically influencing the town through subtle means in order to extract SCP-4531-2 for their own benefit. The Foundation needs more and more as time goes on, so they manipulate the people into doing worse and worse crimes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i really love this and also i'm not quite sure i get it fully?

+ spoilers
- spoilers

Is the escalation of incidents in the addendum indicating that the people (at least, some of them) of the town are aware of exactly how the SCP works and are deliberately doing horrible stuff with oversight of the Foundation just for the benefits of SCP-4531-3 based on how awful the things they have done are?

God I think it is this is horrifying i love it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Loved this in draft mode, loved this in site mode! Take the updoot, well done♡

item.title: SCP-4531 — Silence encourages the tormentor, never the tormented.
item.content:encoded:
The persistence and escalation of anti-Semitic phenomena…[and that] far from having been eliminated, anti-Semitism is today on the rise in Europe. It appears in a variety of forms and is becoming relatively commonplace.
–Parliamentary Assembly of the Council of Europe, Resolution 1563

This article is about ethics, and the rise of modern day anti-semitism.

Originally, this was a collaboration between myself, SecretCrow, and plaguebearer. The original concept was similar, but not exactly the same as the final draft. We eventually lost interest, and we moved on.

Recently, California tried to integrate an "Ethnic Studies Curriculum" for schools to help promote diversity and highlight the struggles of smaller groups that faced persecution. This would have been a great idea, if not for the fact that Jews were not only not mentioned, but were instead displayed in a negative light, primarily due to the State of Israel.

Along with hundreds of thousands of other people, I was outraged, especially considering how much my people and race went through all throughout history. Excluding us was almost like pointing a collective middle finger at us and telling us to fuck off. The course is being redesigned due to backlash, but the fact that it even happened is a major telling sign at the way Jews are seen in the modern day, not including many, many other things.

In my anger, I remembered the old 2018 draft I had with Crow and Plague. I scrolled to the page, reread what I wrote, and rewrote what I read. I calmed down later, went back, rephrased the crazed ramblings, and then finished it.

I'm not sure what compelled me to make the Foundation the way it was, but I feel like it best stands as an analogy — of what, you can probably fill in the gaps.

However, I will say that I always felt the concept of "cold, not cruel" was intrinsically flawed. There are no absolute goods, and there are no absolute evils.

Besides, what is cruelty, if it's for a good cause?

Department of Tactical Theology Logo created by Aethris.

river.jpg
